identity.short_name: Double-root cubic count -> reciprocal quartic triple-square sum

subdomain(s): rational root theorem and Vieta relations

problem_spec.summary: Composite (CHAIN) family: compute bridge quantity T from component A (Count bounded cubics with exactly one double root and both roots in (2,2)); then use that T as a required parameter in component B (Triple products of squared roots via reciprocal Newton sums (quartic)) to determine the final output (reduced mod 1000). Instance summary: Count monic cubics with a prescribed double root and simple root in {-1,0,1} to get T, then compute a reciprocal quartic triple-product-of-squares sum via reciprocal Newton identities, reduced mod 1000.
givens: Cubic p has a double root r in {-1,0,1} and a simple root s in {-1,0,1}, rs.
T is the number of such cubics.

parameter(s).name: root_set
parameter(s).purpose: limits cubic roots to a finite set

parameter(s).name: target_interval
parameter(s).purpose: required location of the distinct real roots.

parameter(s).name: multiplicity_pattern
parameter(s).purpose: enforces exactly two distinct roots for a cubic.

parameter(s).name: degree
parameter(s).purpose: fixes Newton-identity depth and the symmetric-expression arity (triples of 4 roots).

parameter(s).name: coefficients
parameter(s).domain: (a1,a2,a3,a4)  ^4 for a monic quartic P(x)=x^4+a1 x^3+a2 x^2+a3 x+a4, with a40
parameter(s).purpose: controls the root symmetric sums; nonzero constant term enables reciprocal-root methods.

parameter(s).name: T_bridge
parameter(s).domain: derived integer computed from component A invariants
parameter(s).purpose: interface quantity produced by component A and consumed by component B (non-stapled coupling).

invariant(s).name: reciprocal_quartic_inversion_closure
invariant(s).statement: Q_T is palindromic so roots are closed under u->1/u.

invariant(s).name: multiplicity_characterization
invariant(s).statement: A real monic cubic has exactly two distinct real roots iff it factors as (xr)^2(xs) with rs real, equivalently iff gcd(P,P) has degree 1 (or discriminant =0 but P not a perfect cube).

invariant(s).name: double_root_rational_then_integer
invariant(s).statement: The double root r is a root of gcd(P,P), hence rational; since P is monic with integer coefficients, the Rational Root Theorem forces r to be an integer dividing c.

invariant(s).name: interval_forces_small_root_set
invariant(s).statement: With r integer and r(2,2), we must have r{1,0,1}. Vieta then gives s=a2r, so s is also an integer and s(2,2) forces s{1,0,1}.

invariant(s).name: coefficient_reconstruction_from_roots
invariant(s).statement: If P(x)=(xr)^2(xs), then (a,b,c)=(-(2r+s), r^2+2rs, r^2 s). Thus each admissible ordered pair (r,s) yields exactly one coefficient triple.

invariant(s).name: complementary_product_identity
invariant(s).statement: For roots r1..r4 with product e4=a4, we have _{i<j<k} r_i^2 r_j^2 r_k^2 = e4^2 _{i=1}^4 1/r_i^2.

invariant(s).name: reciprocal_polynomial_roots
invariant(s).statement: If a40, then s_i=1/r_i are roots of the monic reciprocal polynomial Q(x) = (1/a4) x^4 P(1/x) = x^4 + (a3/a4)x^3 + (a2/a4)x^2 + (a1/a4)x + (1/a4).

invariant(s).name: newton_girard_for_p2
invariant(s).statement: For monic Q(x)=x^4+A1 x^3+A2 x^2+A3 x+A4 with roots s_i and p_k= s_i^k, Newton gives p1=-A1 and p2 = A1^2 - 2A2, uniquely determined by (A1,A2).

invariant(s).name: coefficient_only_closed_form
invariant(s).statement: Combining the reciprocal transform and Newton for p2 yields T = a4^2 \cdot p2 = a4^2 \cdot ((a3/a4)^2 - 2(a2/a4)) = a3^2 - 2 a2 a4, so T is a coefficient polynomial independent of a1.

invariant(s).name: bridge_interface_invariant
invariant(s).statement: The composite is coupled by the interface variable T: component A uniquely determines T, and component B is defined/solved only after substituting this specific T into its parameter slot.

essential_assumption(s): Distinctness rs ensures degree 3 with exactly one repeated root.
The interval is open; roots equal to ±2 are excluded.
